annotations.label: Field extension

anchor.snippet: a field extension is a pair of fields

annotations.note: Field extensions K ⊆ L are encoded as `Algebra K L` with both as fields; the `Algebra` class generalizes to commutative semirings.

annotations.label: Complex numbers over reals

anchor.snippet: the complex numbers are an extension field of the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib provides the ℝ-algebra (hence field extension) structure on ℂ.

annotations.label: Subfield

anchor.snippet: is a subset

annotations.note: `Subfield K` is a structure extending `Subring K` for a `DivisionRing K`.

annotations.label: Subfield shares zero element

anchor.snippet: the latter definition implies

annotations.note: `SubfieldClass` requires containing 0 and 1 (inherited via `SubringClass`), so the zero element is shared by definition.

annotations.label: Rationals as subfield

anchor.snippet: the field of rational numbers is a subfield of the real numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib gives ℚ as a subfield/subring of ℝ via the canonical (injective) algebraMap.

annotations.label: Characteristic of subfield

anchor.snippet: The characteristic of a subfield is the same as the characteristic of the larger field

annotations.note: Injective ring homs transport `CharP`, so any subfield inherits the ambient field's characteristic.

annotations.label: Extension field

anchor.snippet: is an extension field or simply extension of

annotations.note: Same `Algebra K L` encoding used throughout Mathlib for field extensions.

annotations.label: Intermediate field

anchor.snippet: is said to be an intermediate field

annotations.note: `IntermediateField K L` is a subfield of L containing the image of K.

annotations.label: Degree of extension

anchor.snippet: The dimension of this vector space is called the degree of the extension

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `Module.finrank K L` (or `Module.rank`) for the degree [L:K].

annotations.label: Trivial, quadratic, cubic, finite extensions

anchor.snippet: The degree of an extension is 1 if and only if the two fields are equal

annotations.note: Finite extensions are `FiniteDimensional K L`; specific degrees (1,2,3) are captured via `Module.finrank = n` predicates.

annotations.label: Multiplicativity of degree

anchor.snippet: is finite if and only if both

annotations.note: States `finrank F K * finrank K A = finrank F A`, the tower law. (Anchor tightened from generic 'the extension' for uniqueness.)

annotations.label: Field generated, adjoin, simple extension

anchor.snippet: there is a smallest subfield of

annotations.note: `IntermediateField.adjoin F S` is the smallest intermediate field containing S.

annotations.label: Adjunction

anchor.snippet: often said to result from the adjunction of

annotations.note: Adjunction K(S) is exactly `IntermediateField.adjoin K S` with notation `F⟮x⟯` for the simple case.

annotations.label: Primitive element theorem

anchor.snippet: In characteristic 0, every finite extension is a simple extension

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the stronger statement for finite separable extensions (which covers char 0).

annotations.label: Infinite simple extension is rational fractions

anchor.snippet: If a simple extension

annotations.note: `RatFunc K` formalizes the field of rational fractions, but I did not find a packaged isomorphism `F⟮x⟯ ≃ RatFunc F` when x is transcendental.

annotations.label: Abstract field extension as injective homomorphism

anchor.snippet: one abstractly defines a field extension as an injective ring homomorphism between two fields

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Algebra K L` between fields gives a (necessarily injective) ring hom `algebraMap K L`.

annotations.label: Ring homomorphisms between fields are injective

anchor.snippet: Every ring homomorphism between fields is injective

annotations.note: `RingHom.injective` shows any ring hom from a division ring (incl. fields) into a nontrivial ring is injective.

annotations.label: C over R over Q

anchor.snippet: The field of complex numbers

annotations.note: ℂ/ℝ/ℚ tower is fully populated via existing `Algebra` and `IsScalarTower` instances.

annotations.label: Quadratic extension of Q

anchor.snippet: is an extension field of

annotations.note: Quadratic extensions like ℚ(√d) are realized via `Zsqrtd` and number-field machinery, with general adjoin available.

annotations.label: Degree 4 extension of Q

anchor.snippet: is an extension field of both

annotations.note: Such specific compositums like ℚ(√2,√3) can be constructed via `adjoin` but I found no named instance for this concrete degree-4 example.

annotations.label: Algebraic number fields and p-adics

anchor.snippet: Finite extensions of

annotations.note: Number fields are `NumberField`; p-adic completions are `Padic` in `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Padics.PadicNumbers`.

annotations.label: Quotient ring construction

anchor.snippet: It is common to construct an extension field of a given field

annotations.note: `AdjoinRoot f := K[X] ⧸ (f)` realizes the standard quotient-by-irreducible construction.

annotations.label: Splitting field

anchor.snippet: one can construct a splitting field of any polynomial

annotations.note: `SplittingField f` is the constructed splitting field; `IsSplittingField` axiomatizes it.

annotations.label: Finite field extension

anchor.snippet: there is a unique (up to isomorphism) finite field

annotations.note: `GaloisField p n` is the field of p^n elements, and `algEquivGaloisField` gives uniqueness up to iso.

annotations.label: Rational functions field

anchor.snippet: we can consider the field

annotations.note: `RatFunc K` is the field of rational functions over K.

annotations.label: Meromorphic functions on Riemann surface

anchor.snippet: the set of all meromorphic functions defined on M is a field

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl packages the meromorphic functions on a Riemann surface as a field.

annotations.label: Algebraic element and minimal polynomial

anchor.snippet: is algebraic over K if it is a root of a nonzero polynomial with coefficients in K

annotations.note: `IsAlgebraic` predicate plus `minpoly` (Mathlib.FieldTheory.Minpoly.Basic) provide both notions.

annotations.label: Algebraic iff simple extension finite

anchor.snippet: An element s of L is algebraic over K if and only if the simple extension

annotations.note: Algebraicity of x is equivalent to `FiniteDimensional K K⟮x⟯` via this and related lemmas.

annotations.label: Algebraic closure in L

anchor.snippet: The set of the elements of L that are algebraic over K form a subextension

annotations.note: `algebraicClosure F E` is the intermediate field of elements of E algebraic over F.

annotations.label: Algebraic extension

anchor.snippet: is an extension such that every element of L is algebraic over K

annotations.note: Typeclass `Algebra.IsAlgebraic K L` asserts every element of L is algebraic over K.

annotations.label: Simple algebraic iff finite

anchor.snippet: A simple extension is algebraic if and only if it is finite

annotations.note: Combined with `adjoin_simple_eq_top_iff_of_isAlgebraic` this gives the simple-algebraic ⇔ finite equivalence.

annotations.label: Every finite extension is algebraic

anchor.snippet: every finite extension is algebraic

annotations.note: `Algebra.IsAlgebraic.of_finite` (or the instance derived from `FiniteDimensional`) gives that every finite extension is algebraic.

annotations.label: Existence of algebraic closure

anchor.snippet: Every field K has an algebraic closure

annotations.note: `AlgebraicClosure k` exists with instances `IsAlgClosed` and `IsAlgClosure`.

annotations.label: Algebraically independent, transcendence degree, transcendence basis

anchor.snippet: is called algebraically independent over K if no non-trivial polynomial relation

annotations.note: `AlgebraicIndependent`, `IsTranscendenceBasis` and `Algebra.trdeg` (via `IsTranscendenceBasis.lift_cardinalMk_eq`) cover all three notions.

annotations.label: Purely transcendental extension

anchor.snippet: is said to be purely transcendental if and only if there exists a transcendence basis

annotations.note: No `IsPurelyTranscendental` predicate was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Purely transcendental extension example

anchor.snippet: For example, consider the extension

annotations.note: Multivariate rational function fields exist (`MvRatFunc`), but the concept of "purely transcendental example" is not packaged as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Function fields of rational varieties

anchor.snippet: Purely transcendental extensions of an algebraically closed field occur as function fields of rational varieties

annotations.note: Rational varieties and their function fields are not formalized in Mathlib at this level.

annotations.label: Normal extension

anchor.snippet: is called normal if every irreducible polynomial in K

annotations.note: `Normal F K` extends `Algebra.IsAlgebraic` with `Splits ((minpoly F x).map ...)` for all x.

annotations.label: Normal closure

anchor.snippet: admits a normal closure

annotations.note: `normalClosure K L M` is the minimal normal intermediate field of `M/K` containing the image of L.

annotations.label: Separable and Galois extension

anchor.snippet: is called separable if the minimal polynomial of every element of L over K is separable

annotations.note: `Algebra.IsSeparable` and `IsGalois` (Mathlib.FieldTheory.Galois.Basic) formalize separability and the Galois property.

annotations.label: Finite separable has primitive element

anchor.snippet: A consequence of the primitive element theorem states that every finite separable extension has a primitive element

annotations.note: Exact statement of the primitive element theorem for finite separable extensions.

annotations.label: Galois group and abelian extension

anchor.snippet: we can consider its automorphism group

annotations.note: The Galois group `L ≃ₐ[K] L` and `IsGalois` are formalized; "abelian extension" isn't a named predicate but is expressible via `IsCommutative` on the Galois group.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of Galois theory

anchor.snippet: there is a bijection between the intermediate fields and the subgroups of the Galois group

annotations.note: Bijection between intermediate fields and subgroups, with `IntermediateFieldEquivClosedSubgroup` in the infinite case.

annotations.label: Central simple algebra

anchor.snippet: A closer non-commutative analog are central simple algebras

annotations.note: `Algebra.IsCentral` plus `IsSimpleRing` express the central simple algebra notion; used in `Mathlib.Algebra.BrauerGroup.Defs`.

annotations.label: Quaternions as CSA over reals

anchor.snippet: the quaternions are a central simple algebra over the reals

annotations.note: `Quaternion ℝ` (i.e. ℍ) is constructed; central simple structure follows via `Algebra.IsCentral` and `IsSimpleRing` instances.

annotations.label: Brauer equivalence / Brauer group

anchor.snippet: Brauer equivalent

annotations.note: `BrauerGroup k` packages CSAs over k modulo Brauer equivalence.

annotations.label: Extension of scalars by complexification

anchor.snippet: given a real vector space, one can produce a complex vector space via complexification

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes extension of scalars/base change as `TensorProduct R S M` with full algebra structure; complexification is the special case S = ℂ.
